untranslatable_reason: This signal requires specialized derivatives pricing indicators not in the allowed list: (1) theoretical spread valuation based on financing costs, (2) spread mispricing quantification relative to theoretical fair value, (3) financing cost differential calculation, (4) bid-ask spread on calendar spreads. The allowed indicators are limited to price action, momentum, and volatility metrics. Calendar spread pricing requires multi-leg derivative valuation, carry cost modeling, and term structure analysis that cannot be expressed using standard technical indicators.
translation_notes: Signal NATENBERG_DRY_60_0 describes a quantitative arbitrage strategy for calendar spreads based on cost-of-carry financing. While the logic (buy discount → wait for mean reversion to fair value) could theoretically map to a reversion pattern, the entry/exit triggers depend entirely on derivative-specific valuation metrics (financing costs, theoretical pricing, basis convergence) that are outside the scope of technical analysis indicators. This requires a specialized options/futures analytics engine rather than a standard price-action backtest.
